2603.26292.title: findsylls: A Language-Agnostic Toolkit for Syllable-Level Speech Tokenization and Embedding
2603.26292.paper_summary: Syllable-level units offer compact and linguistically meaningful representations for spoken language modeling and unsupervised word discovery, but research on syllabification remains fragmented across disparate implementations, datasets, and evaluation protocols. We introduce findsylls, a modular, language-agnostic toolkit that unifies classical syllable detectors and end-to-end syllabifiers under a common interface for syllable segmentation, embedding extraction, and multi-granular evaluation. The toolkit implements and standardizes widely used methods (e.g., Sylber, VG-HuBERT) and allows their components to be recombined, enabling controlled comparisons of representations, algorithms, and token rates. We demonstrate findsylls on English and Spanish corpora and on new hand-annotated data from Kono, an underdocumented Central Mande language, illustrating how a single framework can support reproducible syllable-level experiments across both high-resource and under-resourced settings.
2603.26292.paper_summary_zh: 音節級單位為口語語言建模和無監督單詞發現提供了緊湊且具有語言意義的表示，但對音節劃分的研究仍然在不同的實現、數據集和評估協議之間分散。我們介紹了 findsylls，一個模組化的、與語言無關的工具包，統一了經典的音節檢測器和端到端的音節劃分器，提供了一個共同的介面，用於音節分割、嵌入提取和多粒度評估。該工具包實現並標準化了廣泛使用的方法（例如，Sylber、VG-HuBERT），並允許其組件重新組合，使得對表示、算法和標記速率的控制比較成為可能。我們在英語和西班牙語語料庫以及來自 Kono 的新手工標註數據上演示了 findsylls，這是一種記錄不足的中部曼德語，展示了單一框架如何支持在高資源和資源不足的環境中可重複的音節級實驗。
2603.26292.author: Héctor Javier Vázquez Martínez et.al.
